title: Bourreria uniflora
intro: Bourreria uniflora, also known as the 'Wild Coffee Tree', is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive, single-flowered inflorescence. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its use in treating digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and diarrhea, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Bourreria uniflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and fever.
taxonomy: Bourreria uniflora, a species of flowering plant, is classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family is commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family and consists of around 200 genera. Within the Boraginaceae family, Bourreria uniflora is placed in the genus Bourreria, which comprises about 30 species.
